data.title: As a DSO plugin user, I can run `dso update-artifacts` to sync all stale artifacts without losing my customizations
data.description:
## Description

**What**: An explicit `dso update-artifacts` command (`update-artifacts.sh`) that compares host project artifacts against the current plugin version and applies per-artifact surgical updates, preserving user customizations and escalating unresolvable conflicts as structured JSON.

**Why**: The session-start notification (S2) tells users what is stale; this story provides the action surface to fix it. The update must be non-destructive — user customizations in config files and CI workflows must be preserved, with unresolvable conflicts escalated rather than silently overwritten.

**Scope**:
- IN: `update-artifacts.sh` script callable as `dso update-artifacts` via the host shim dispatch table
- IN: Per-artifact strategies: shim=overwrite, dso-config.conf=key-additive merge, .pre-commit-config.yaml=existing `merge_precommit_hooks` logic, .github/workflows/ci.yml=new `merge_ci_workflow` function
- IN: Key-additive config merge: treats commented-out keys as user-intentional exclusions (present, not absent); `# dso-version:` is reserved metadata and must be updated, never treated as a user exclusion
- IN: Exit code 2 + JSON stdout on unresolvable merge conflict: `{"artifact": "<name>", "conflict_ours": "<base64 no-wrap>", "conflict_theirs": "<base64 no-wrap>"}` with platform-detected no-wrap flag (`-w 0` Linux, `-b 0` macOS)
- IN: Shim dispatch table updated to route `update-artifacts` subcommand to `plugins/dso/scripts/update-artifacts.sh`
- OUT: Presenting the conflict JSON to the user in formatted form — the JSON output protocol is the deliverable; caller integration is a future concern
- OUT: Plugin version sync — the user must run `claude plugin sync` before `dso update-artifacts`

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `dso update-artifacts` detects which of the 4 artifacts are stale by reading their version stamps, applies the appropriate per-artifact merge strategy, and updates the version stamp to the current plugin version on success
  ← Satisfies: "Users can sync stale artifacts without losing customizations"
- When this story is complete, dso-config.conf merge appends new keys from the plugin template while leaving existing keys unchanged; commented-out keys are treated as present (user-intentional exclusions); `# dso-version:` is updated as reserved metadata, never suppressed as a commented-out key
  ← Satisfies: "Config merge preserves user customizations"
- When this story is complete, when an artifact's auto-merge fails, `update-artifacts.sh` exits with code 2 and writes JSON to stdout: `{"artifact": "<name>", "conflict_ours": "<base64>", "conflict_theirs": "<base64>"}` using `-w 0` on Linux and `-b 0` on macOS (platform-detected via `uname -s`)
  ← Satisfies: "Unresolvable conflicts are escalated rather than silently overwritten"
- When this story is complete, the host shim dispatch table includes `update-artifacts` routed to `plugins/dso/scripts/update-artifacts.sh`, and calling `dso update-artifacts` from a host project invokes the script successfully
  ← Satisfies: "Users can run `dso update-artifacts` via the standard shim interface"
- When this story is complete, `tests/scripts/test-update-artifacts.sh` covers: clean update for each artifact type, key-additive config merge, commented-out key treated as present, `# dso-version:` reserved metadata handling, CI merge success, CI merge conflict (exit 2 + JSON output), base64 platform detection (macOS and Linux branches), and shim dispatch routing
  ← Satisfies: "Update command is reliable and correct across platforms"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD Done-of-Done

## Considerations

- [Reliability] `merge_ci_workflow` must re-inject the `x-dso-version` YAML key after PyYAML round-trip serialization (PyYAML strips `#` comments; top-level keys survive)
- [Reliability] Platform detection for base64: `uname -s` returns "Darwin" on macOS (use `-b 0`) and "Linux" on Linux (use `-w 0`)
- [Testing] Integration tests must use an isolated temp directory; do not operate on the live host project during tests

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
